title: _elementtree: concurrent access to a shared Element lazily initializes self->extra (create_extra) with no lock -> write/write + write/read data race (Modules/_elementtree.c)
description: xml.etree.ElementTree Element stores its attributes + children in a lazily-allocated `ElementObjectExtra` struct at self->extra, created on first need. create_extra (Modules/_elementtree.c:274) does `self->extra = PyMem_Malloc(...)` with NO per-object critical section, and its callers guard it with an unsynchronized `if (!self->extra) create_extra(self, ...)` (e.g. element_attrib_getter, the `.attrib` getter, _elementtree.c:2083). When a single Element is shared across threads and more than one first-touches it (reads .attrib, calls len(), appends, etc.), two threads both observe self->extra == NULL, both run create_extra, and both WRITE self->extra -> a write/write data race (and a leak: one malloc'd extra is overwritten and orphaned). The write also races concurrent readers of self->extra / self->extra->length (element_length) and clear_extra. This is a lazy-init-without-locking free-threading bug in the _elementtree C accelerator, distinct from the builtin-iterator cursor family.
sites: Modules/_elementtree.c create_extra:274 (WRITE self->extra = PyMem_Malloc(sizeof(ElementObjectExtra)); no critical section)
Modules/_elementtree.c element_attrib_getter:2083 (the `.attrib` getter: unsynchronized `if (!self->extra) create_extra(self, NULL)` lazy init)
Modules/_elementtree.c element_length (READ self->extra / self->extra->length for __len__) and clear_extra (frees/reads self->extra) -- the racing accessors
race_kind: lazy initialization without synchronization: an unlocked `if (!self->extra) create_extra(...)` double-initializes / races the self->extra pointer across threads (write/write + write/read + leak). NOT the iterator-cursor class -- a shared mutable-object field with no per-object critical section.
shared_state: ElementObject.extra (the lazily-allocated ElementObjectExtra* holding attrib + children[]) on a single shared Element
severity: moderate-to-high (write/write on a heap pointer -> leak of one ElementObjectExtra at minimum, and a torn/overwritten self->extra -> potential use of a half-initialized or freed extra struct -> attribute/child corruption or crash under concurrency). Element is a common shared object (a parsed tree shared across worker threads), so the sharing pattern is realistic -- higher real-world priority than the shared-iterator races.
repro_reliability: deterministic: repro.py (8 threads each reading `.attrib` and len() on ONE freshly-created shared Element whose extra is still NULL, x200 rounds) exits 66 on the debug-ft-nojit-tsan build; TSan reports create_extra:274 as the write, reached via element_attrib_getter. Fleet-10 independently drove the create_extra|element_length and clear_extra|create_extra faces (3 veh).
suggested_fix: Take the Element's per-object critical section (Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self)) around the `if (!self->extra) create_extra(...)` lazy-init (and re-check self->extra inside it), in every getter/mutator that lazily creates extra (element_attrib_getter, element_length/subscript, SubElement/append, set, etc.). Or make the lazy init a one-shot compare-exchange on self->extra. The bug is the check-then-create being non-atomic across all these entry points.
confirmed_build: debug-ft-nojit-tsan (CPython 3.16.0a0, --disable-gil --with-thread-sanitizer); also reproduced on release-ft-nojit-tsan (release + TSan) -> NOT a debug-only artifact
related: ALREADY REPORTED upstream: gh-149816 ('22 free-threading race conditions') enumerates the unsynchronized `_elementtree` `extra`/`attrib` races, and OPEN PR #149918 ('gh-149816: Fix race conditions in Modules/_elementtree.c') is the comprehensive fix -- it wraps create_extra's field-init, element_attrib_getter's `if (!self->extra) create_extra(...)`, element_get_attrib, element_length, clear_extra, and traverse in Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self). That is exactly this race. Distinct from the builtin-iterator cursor family (TSAN-0037/0038/0039/0040/0026), and from the other _elementtree FT entries TSAN-0022 (SetEvents) / TSAN-0031 (TreeBuilder).
notes: NOT a new find -- this is gh-149816 / PR #149918 (OPEN, unmerged at the fleet build a1d580430c8), independently reproduced by fusil --tsan (fleet-10, 3 veh) with a minimal isolated repro (shared Element + concurrent .attrib/len -> exit 66 on both debug and release TSan builds). Value: confirms the fix is still needed on current main and that the create_extra WRITE/write (lazy-init) + attrib-getter faces reproduce, not just the read faces gh-149816 lists. No new filing needed; a confirmation comment on #149816/#149918 (that fusil reproduces it and the PR resolves it) is the only outward-facing step, at the maintainer's discretion. When PR #149918 merges, re-run repro.py to move status -> fixed.
